questions.question: In a three-tier campus design, which tier connects end-user devices?
questions.explanation: Access switches connect PCs, phones, and APs.

correct.explanation: Access switches connect PCs, phones, and APs.

incorrect.explanation: Core misses what this question tests. Access switches connect PCs, phones, and APs.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Core" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Distribution misses what this question tests. Access switches connect PCs, phones, and APs.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Distribution" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Spine misses what this question tests. Access switches connect PCs, phones, and APs.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Spine" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: More tiers always mean faster networks — Tiers organize scale and policy; extra hops can add latency if over-engineered

questions.question: Spine-leaf architecture is primarily used for…
questions.explanation: Spine-leaf fabrics optimize predictable, scalable traffic between servers in data centers.

correct.explanation: Spine-leaf fabrics optimize predictable, scalable traffic between servers in data centers.

incorrect.explanation: SOHO Wi‑Fi misses what this question tests. Spine-leaf fabrics optimize predictable, scalable traffic between servers in data centers.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "SOHO Wi‑Fi" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Dial-up WAN misses what this question tests. Spine-leaf fabrics optimize predictable, scalable traffic between servers in data centers.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Dial-up WAN" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Home gaming misses what this question tests. Spine-leaf fabrics optimize predictable, scalable traffic between servers in data centers.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Home gaming" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Calling spine-leaf a WAN topology — Spine-leaf is a data-center switching fabric, not a branch WAN pattern

questions.question: Ten branch offices send all WAN traffic through headquarters. Which topology is this?
questions.explanation: All spokes via one hub = hub-and-spoke.

correct.explanation: All spokes via one hub = hub-and-spoke.

incorrect.explanation: Full mesh misses what this question tests. All spokes via one hub = hub-and-spoke.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Full mesh" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Spine-leaf misses what this question tests. All spokes via one hub = hub-and-spoke.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Spine-leaf" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Point-to-point only misses what this question tests. All spokes via one hub = hub-and-spoke.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Point-to-point only" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Assuming hub-and-spoke has automatic redundancy — The hub is a single point of failure unless spokes are dual-homed or meshed

questions.question: What is the main trade-off of a full-mesh WAN?
questions.explanation: Full mesh connects every site to every other — resilient but expensive.

correct.explanation: Full mesh connects every site to every other — resilient but expensive.

incorrect.explanation: No redundancy reflects a common trap: Assuming hub-and-spoke has automatic redundancy. The hub is a single point of failure unless spokes are dual-homed or meshed.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Assuming hub-and-spoke has automatic redundancy

incorrect.explanation: Only works with fiber misses what this question tests. Full mesh connects every site to every other — resilient but expensive.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Only works with fiber" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Eliminates routing misses what this question tests. Full mesh connects every site to every other — resilient but expensive.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Eliminates routing" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Assuming hub-and-spoke has automatic redundancy — The hub is a single point of failure unless spokes are dual-homed or meshed

questions.question: A collapsed core campus design has how many tiers?
questions.explanation: Two-tier merges distribution and core — access + collapsed distribution/core.

correct.explanation: Two-tier merges distribution and core — access + collapsed distribution/core.

incorrect.explanation: One misses what this question tests. Two-tier merges distribution and core — access + collapsed distribution/core.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "One" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Four misses what this question tests. Two-tier merges distribution and core — access + collapsed distribution/core.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Four" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Five misses what this question tests. Two-tier merges distribution and core — access + collapsed distribution/core.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Five" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: More tiers always mean faster networks — Tiers organize scale and policy; extra hops can add latency if over-engineered

questions.question: A home user has one wireless router providing NAT, DHCP, and Wi‑Fi. This is typical of…
questions.explanation: Small office/home office networks are simple, often one integrated gateway.

correct.explanation: Small office/home office networks are simple, often one integrated gateway.

incorrect.explanation: Core datacenter misses what this question tests. Small office/home office networks are simple, often one integrated gateway.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Core datacenter" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Full mesh WAN misses what this question tests. Small office/home office networks are simple, often one integrated gateway.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Full mesh WAN" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Spine fabric misses what this question tests. Small office/home office networks are simple, often one integrated gateway.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Spine fabric" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Cloud means no local network — Hybrid designs keep on-prem LAN/WAN while using cloud for apps or compute

questions.question: In which cloud model does the customer manage the operating system but not the physical hardware?

incorrect.explanation: On-prem only misses what this question tests. IaaS provides VMs/infrastructure; the customer manages OS and above.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "On-prem only" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Cloud means no local network — Hybrid designs keep on-prem LAN/WAN while using cloud for apps or compute

questions.question: True or False: In spine-leaf, each leaf switch connects to every spine switch.
questions.explanation: True — full mesh between leaves and spines is the defining property.

correct.explanation: True — full mesh between leaves and spines is the defining property.

incorrect.explanation: This statement is False, but the tested fact is: True — full mesh between leaves and spines is the defining property.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "False" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Calling spine-leaf a WAN topology — Spine-leaf is a data-center switching fabric, not a branch WAN pattern
